reasoning: The chart fails to answer the specific business question for several reasons: 1) The question asks for revenue in the years 2021-2024 (implying an annual aggregation or comparison), but the chart displays monthly data points overlaid on top of each other without clear distinction by year. This makes it impossible to see the total yearly trend clearly. 2) The y-axis limit is set to 160,000, which cuts off the highest data point (June 2022 at ~242k), making that critical peak invisible and misleading. 3) The legend labels are generic ('Label of data') instead of specifying the years (2021, 2022, etc.), so the user cannot distinguish which line belongs to which year. 4) A simple bar chart showing total annual revenue would be much more appropriate for answering 'How much revenue was there...'.
suggested_improvement: Change the chart type to a bar chart aggregating sales by Year (summing monthly values). Ensure the Y-axis scale accommodates the maximum value (approx. 900k+ for 2022). If keeping the line chart, separate lines must have distinct labels (e.g., '2021', '2022') and the Y-axis must extend beyond 250,000.
